_meta.doctrine: The operator's hand is the source of truth. Agent drafts unblock implementation; the operator promotes them by rewriting and changing _tag to OPERATOR-VOICE. verify:phos-voice fails if any OPERATOR-VOICE line is silently rewritten by an agent. (no generatedAt: deterministic build for drift detection; git log + lock file are the audit trail.)

_default._tagSource: fallback for any page without its own entry
_default.hint: Looking for something? The context card adapts everything to how your brain works. The lab shows what we build. Or just keep exploring.

/bonding._tagSource: cross-origin surface; bridge ships in BUS4 Phase 3
/bonding.hint: A chemistry game for parents and kids. Drag atoms, build molecules, play together — even from different rooms.
/bonding.fallback: Works best on a tablet. Grab a kid if you have one nearby.

/buffer.hint: Drafts and routes communication based on what's in your context card.
/buffer.fallback: If you haven't made a context card yet, the buffer works in default mode.

/dome._tagSource: highest-stimulation page; keep voice deliberately understated
/dome.hint: Every node is something we built. Every line is a connection between them. This is the whole system in one view.
/dome.fallback: It's a lot to take in. Zoom into whatever catches your eye, or head to the lab for the plain-text version.

/ede.hint: Every tool, one keystroke away. Built for the operator's hands.
/ede.fallback: If you're exploring without context, the lab is the friendlier door.

/glass-box.greeting: Everything we do, in the open.
/glass-box.hint: Verifiers, deploys, audits, ledgers. If P31 ran it, it shows here.
/glass-box.fallback: Hard read? The support page has the same numbers in plain English.

/lab.hint: Everything here is free. Everything is open source. If something helps you, take it. If something breaks, tell us.
/lab.fallback: Not sure where to start? The context card is usually the best first step. After that, BONDING is the most fun.

/ops._tagSource: operator-only surface; PHOS still greets in case someone is shoulder-surfing
/ops.hint: Fleet health, filing deadlines, board status, grant pipeline. Everything the operator needs on one screen.
/ops.fallback: If you're not the operator and you're seeing this — hi. You probably have a context card with operator access. That's fine. Look around.

/passport._tagSource: operator: this is the card itself — write what feels true
/passport.hint:
Your context card tells every page on this site how to work for you. Font size, contrast, motion, color — you set it once, it follows you everywhere.

Nothing leaves your browser. We literally cannot see what you put here.
/passport.fallback: Skip anything that doesn't feel right. Everything still works without it. This just makes it work better.

/research.hint: 22 open-access research papers. The theory behind the tools — published so anyone can check our work, cite it, or build on it. All free. All permanent.
/research.fallback: Heavy reading. Come back when you have the spoons for it. The tools don't require any of this to use.

/stylebook.hint: This is how P31 looks, reads, and feels — documented so anyone building with us stays consistent. Tokens, typography, color, voice rules, accessibility targets.
/stylebook.fallback: If you're here to contribute, start with the token reference. If you're here because you're curious, welcome to the kitchen.

/support._tagSource: line 1 mirrors operator framing — keep it
/support.hint: P31 runs on zero salary and whatever people can give. Every dollar goes straight to building tools for neurodivergent families. We take 0% platform fees — if you give ten bucks, we get ten bucks.
/support.fallback: Not in a position to give? That's fine. Using the tools and telling one person about them is just as real.

/welcome._tagSource: §3.2 first derivation — locked by verify:phos-voice SHA; operator-approved tagline change 2026-05-01 evening
/welcome.hint:
For every family out there figuring it out as they go — help is on the way.

Start with your context card. Two minutes. It makes everything here work better for you.
/welcome.fallback: Or just look around. I'll be here if you need me.
